meta.title: Blood Alcohol Calculator — BAC with Widmark Formula
meta.description: Calculate your blood alcohol concentration (BAC) with the Widmark formula. Time until sober, legal limits and influencing factors. Free, instant, no sign-up.

bac.title: Blood Alcohol Calculator
bac.description: Calculate your blood alcohol concentration based on the Widmark formula.
bac.belowLegalLimit: You are below the German legal limit of 0.5‰.
bac.aboveLegalLimit: You are above the German legal limit of 0.5‰. Do not drive!
bac.effectModerate: Noticeable impairment of coordination and reaction time
bac.howItWorksText: This calculator uses the Widmark formula: Alcohol (g) = Volume × (ABV / 100) × 0.8. BAC = Alcohol (g) / (Body weight × r), where r = 0.68 (men) or 0.55 (women). Elimination rate is approximately 0.15‰ per hour. This is an estimate — individual factors may vary.

faq.a: Using the Widmark formula: alcohol (g) / (body weight × r). r = 0.68 for men, 0.55 for women. This estimate is medically established but varies individually.

faq.q: How fast does the body metabolize alcohol?
faq.a: About 0.015–0.02 % per hour, on average 0.015 % BAC. Metabolism cannot be sped up by coffee, water, or exercise.

faq.q: What are the legal BAC limits?
faq.a: Most U.S. states: 0.08 % BAC. Many European countries: 0.05 %. Germany: 0.05 %. New drivers and under 21: typically 0.00 %. Always check local law.
Individual factors like stomach contents, liver function, sex, medications, and body composition can shift the real value by ±20–30 %.

faq.q: Are women more sensitive to alcohol?
faq.a: Yes. Women on average have lower body water and reduced alcohol dehydrogenase activity. For the same intake, BAC is higher.

faq.q: When is it safe to drive again?
faq.a: Only at 0.00 % BAC. After reaching 0.10 %, full clearance takes about 6–7 hours. Residual alcohol the next morning is common — even after 8 hours of sleep.
